Main_Finding: Scaling masked-LM pretraining (ESM-1) to 250M sequences causes secondary structure, contacts, and function to emerge in learned representations; properties surveyed layer-by-layer.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Establishes that ESM layers carry emergent biological signal — my work asks how these layer-localized signals are jointly recombined into mutation-effect representations, not merely localized.

Main_Finding: Scaling ESM-2 up to 15B parameters causes atomic-level structural information to emerge, enabling single-sequence structure prediction with ESMFold.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Demonstrates structure can be recovered from a final-layer representation alone — my work shows mutation-effect signal is, by contrast, distributed across pairs of layers rather than concentrated in any single layer.

Main_Finding: Trains six large autoencoder / auto-regressive PLMs on UniRef/BFD and probes per-layer embeddings for secondary structure, localization, and membrane prediction.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Provides a per-layer (not per-pair) probing protocol — my analysis extends this from a one-axis (layer index) view to a two-axis (layer pair) view.

Main_Finding: Row/column attention over MSAs achieves SOTA unsupervised contact prediction via tied row-attention.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Cross-position (not cross-layer) attention is the inductive bias — my work studies cross-LAYER, not cross-residue, interactions for mutation effects.

Main_Finding: Shows that representation geometry across PLM layers strongly affects interpretability and downstream transfer; the 'best layer' depends on task and architecture.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Highlights the inadequacy of any single 'best layer' baseline — my work generalizes from 'best layer' to 'best layer pair', revealing structure that cannot be observed by per-layer probing.

Main_Finding: Attention heads in protein Transformers track 3D contacts and binding sites; higher-level biophysical properties are tracked by deeper-layer heads.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Provides depth-stratified interpretation of a SINGLE LM by inspecting heads layer-by-layer — my work extends this depth view to a layer-pair view focused on mutation-effect encoding.

Citation: Rao et al. (Transformer PLMs are unsupervised structure learners)
Main_Finding: A sparse logistic regression over attention heads pooled across ALL ESM layers extracts SOTA unsupervised residue–residue contacts.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Methodologically closest existing approach to multi-layer fusion in PLMs — but for residue contacts, not mutation effects; my work shows that mutation-effect signal requires a structurally similar yet biologically distinct multi-layer combination.

Main_Finding: Releases a 5-task PLM benchmark and probes recurrent/convolutional/Transformer models per task using per-task best-layer features.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Codifies the single-layer probing protocol that my work challenges; my findings imply per-task benchmarks may understate signal carried in layer pairs.

Main_Finding: Sparse autoencoders trained per-layer on ESM-2 expose thousands of interpretable, layer-localized biological features (binding sites, motifs, disordered regions) and superposition.
Relationship_to_My_Work: SAE features are extracted separately within each layer — my work asks the complementary question of how features in different layers interact to encode mutation effects.

Main_Finding: Multimodal generative PLM that jointly tokenizes sequence, structure, and function, enabling prompted generation of novel functional proteins (incl. distant GFP variants).
Relationship_to_My_Work: Scale-up and generative angle on the same backbone family; my work focuses on the INTERPRETATION of an earlier, fully analyzable variant (ESM-2) — they are orthogonal aims.

Main_Finding: ESM-1v's FINAL-layer masked-marginal log-likelihood achieves SOTA zero-shot mutation-effect prediction with no family-specific training.
Relationship_to_My_Work: The canonical 'final layer = mutation score' approach — my work re-examines this assumption and shows the signal is distributed across layer pairs rather than concentrated at layer 33.

Main_Finding: Autoregressive PLM with grouped multi-scale conv attention and inference-time MSA retrieval achieves SOTA fitness prediction, incl. indels and shallow MSAs.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Uses a single output-layer score — my work is not a competing predictor but a representational analysis of where in the network mutation information lives.

Main_Finding: Releases >250 DMS assays plus clinical benchmark; standardizes zero/few-shot evaluation of dozens of PLM/MSA baselines.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Provides the DMS datasets I use, but treats them as a prediction benchmark — my work uses them as encoding-analysis substrates rather than a leaderboard.

Main_Finding: VAE trained on family MSAs classifies pathogenicity of human variants on par with high-throughput experiments; labels >256k VUS.
Relationship_to_My_Work: MSA-based, not a Transformer PLM — included as a non-PLM mutation-effect anchor; my work targets the layer-organization question that is specific to deep Transformers.

Main_Finding: Uses ESM-1b's last-layer log-likelihood to score all ~450M human missense variants, outperforming prior tools on ClinVar/HGMD and 28 DMS datasets.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Closely related ESM-family backbone (ESM-1b in their work; ESM-2 in ours) and same DMS evaluations — my work asks not how well its final layer scores variants, but how mutation information is organized across all 33 layers.

Main_Finding: Simple ridge regression combining evolutionary density-model scores with assay labels outperforms more complex low-N protein fitness predictors.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Emphasizes that linear combinations of compact representations can match deep predictors — supporting (rather than contradicting) the view that mutation-effect signal is geometrically simple and recoverable from layer combinations.

Main_Finding: A scalar-mix probe over all BERT layers shows POS → parsing → NER → SRL → coreference are localized in that order, with feedback between layers.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Methodologically the closest non-PLM analog to my work: explicit multi-layer attribution. Inspires the framing of 'where along the depth axis is information localized, and across which layer pairs is it integrated?'

Citation: Rogers, Kovaleva & Rumshisky (A Primer in BERTology)
Main_Finding: Synthesizes ~150 BERT analyses on how syntactic, semantic, and world knowledge are distributed across layers and heads.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Provides the conceptual vocabulary (low/mid/high layer roles) that I import into PLMs — the project is to give the protein-LM the same kind of layer-level cartography this review compiled for BERT.

Citation: Geva et al. (FFN as key-value memories)
Main_Finding: Transformer feed-forward layers act as key-value memories; lower layers store shallow patterns, upper layers semantic ones, with residual-stream composition across layers.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Mechanistic basis for treating the residual stream as a cross-layer integrator — directly motivates why Early–Late layer pairs (rather than any single layer) could carry mutation-effect signal.

Main_Finding: A learned affine probe per layer decodes intermediate residual-stream states into vocabulary distributions, exposing iterative refinement of predictions across layers.
Relationship_to_My_Work: Layer-by-layer decoding trajectory — methodological analog to mapping mutation-effect signal across the depth of a PLM; supports interpreting mutation encoding as a cross-layer process.
